info.name: Campaign Reward API
info.description:
**Two surfaces:**

**Admin** (`/api/v1/raffles/admin/...`) — requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Log in first via `POST /auth/login` to get a token.

**Participant** (`/api/v1/raffles/campaigns/...`) — public, no auth required.

---

**Admin happy path:**
1. `POST /auth/login` → save token
2. `POST /admin/campaigns` → create campaign
3. `POST /admin/campaigns/:campaignId/booths` → add booths
4. `POST /admin/campaigns/:campaignId/prizes` → add wheel prizes
5. `POST /admin/campaigns/:campaignId/images` → upload site images
6. `PATCH /admin/campaigns/:campaignId` → set status to active

**Participant happy path:**
1. `GET /campaigns/event/:eventTag` — load campaign + booth list
2. Client scans booths locally (no API call per scan)
3. `POST /campaigns/:campaignId/generate-raffle-qr` — submit booth codes → get encrypted QR
4. Staff station: `POST /campaigns/:campaignId/validate-raffle` → get `raffleEntryId`
5. `GET /campaigns/:campaignId/prizes` — load prize list to render wheel
6. Wheel spins client-side → `POST /campaigns/:campaignId/spin-wheel` → send `prizeId` (win) or null (lose)

---

**Seeded campaign ID:** `1` | **Event tag:** `WORLDBEX2026` | **Threshold:** 300 pts

**Survey admin path:**
1. `POST /admin/campaigns/:campaignId/surveys` → create survey
2. `POST .../surveys/:surveyId/questions` → add questions
3. `POST .../questions/:questionId/options` → add options
4. `POST .../questions/:questionId/matrix-rows` → add rows (matrix only)

**Survey visitor path (after QR validation):**
1. `GET /campaigns/:campaignId/surveys/active?triggerEvent=booth_scan` → fetch active survey
2. `POST /campaigns/:campaignId/surveys/:surveyId/submit` → submit answers

variable.description: Auto-filled from Create Campaign or Get Campaign by Event Tag
Auto-filled from Create Booth or set manually
Auto-filled from Create Prize or set manually
Auto-filled from Upload Image or set manually

item.name: A. Auth
item.description: Admin authentication. Call Login first to get a JWT token — it is auto-saved to the `admin_token` variable and sent automatically on all Admin requests.

request.description: Rate-limited. Returns a JWT Bearer token valid for 24h. Token is auto-saved to `admin_token`.

response.name: 200 - Login Success
401 - Invalid Credentials
403 - Account Inactive

request.description: Verify your token is valid and see the authenticated admin's profile.

response.name: 200 - Admin Profile

item.name: B. Admin — Campaign Management
item.description:
Campaign CRUD. All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{admin_token}}`.

Base path: `{{base_url}}/admin/campaigns`

request.description: `campaignId` is auto-saved.
Only campaigns with status=draft can be deleted.

item.name: C. Admin — Prize Management
item.description:
Prize catalog CRUD. All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{admin_token}}`.

Base path: `{{base_url}}/admin/campaigns/:campaignId/prizes`

`prizeId` is auto-saved from Create Prize. The public `GET /campaigns/:campaignId/prizes` endpoint returns active prizes for the wheel.

request.description: Returns all prizes (active and inactive) ordered by sortOrder.

response.name: 200 - Prize List

request.description: `prizeId` is auto-saved. Create a 'No Prize' entry with `isPool: false` for the losing wheel segment.

response.name: 201 - Prize Created

exec: console.log('Updated:', json.data.prize.prizeName, '| qty:', json.data.prize.quantity, '| active:', json.data.prize.isActive);

request.description: Partial update — at least one field required.
Blocked if any claims reference this prize. Set `isActive: false` instead.

response.name: 200 - Deleted
409 - Claims Exist

item.name: D. Admin — Booth Management
item.description:
Booth CRUD. All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{admin_token}}`.

Base path: `{{base_url}}/admin/campaigns/:campaignId/booths`

`boothId` is auto-saved from Create Booth. Default `20260001` matches the seeded first booth.

request.description: Returns ALL booths (active and inactive). Ordered by sortOrder ASC.

response.name: 200 - Booth List
200 - Booth Found

request.description: `qrValue` defaults to `boothCode` if omitted. `boothId` is auto-saved.

response.name: 201 - Booth Created
409 - Duplicate boothCode

exec: console.log('Updated:', json.data.booth.boothCode, '| points:', json.data.booth.points, '| active:', json.data.booth.isActive);

request.description: Partial update — send only the fields to change. At least one field required.

response.name: 200 - Booth Updated

request.description: Blocked if any scan logs reference this booth. Use `PATCH { isActive: false }` instead.

response.name: 200 - Deleted
409 - Scan Logs Exist

item.name: D. Admin — Image Maps
item.description:
Upload and manage site images per campaign. All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{admin_token}}`.

Base path: `{{base_url}}/admin/campaigns/:campaignId/images`

**Upload** uses `multipart/form-data` with the file field named `image`.
**Metadata-only edits** use JSON PATCH.
**File replacement** uses `POST .../replace` (multipart).

request.description: Returns all images grouped by siteCode, ordered by siteCode → sortOrder.

response.name: 200 - Image List

request.description: Multipart form upload. Field name must be `image`. Supported types: jpg, jpeg, png, webp, gif, svg, bmp, tiff. Max 5 MB. `imageId` is auto-saved.

response.name: 201 - Uploaded
400 - No File
400 - File Too Large

request.description: Update metadata only (siteCode, siteName, altText, sortOrder, isActive). Does not replace the file. At least one field required.

formdata.description: New image file to replace the existing one

request.description: Upload a new file to replace the existing one. Old file is deleted from disk. Metadata (siteCode, siteName, etc.) is preserved.
Deletes the DB record and removes the file from disk. Best-effort — request succeeds even if the file was already missing.

response.name: 200 - Deleted

item.name: E. Admin — Stats
item.description: Admin read-only views. All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{admin_token}}`.

request.description: Claim history grouped by prize name + 50 most recent claims.
Full participant view: points (recomputed from scan logs), scans, raffle QR status, claim history. `participantId` is auto-filled from Validate Raffle.

item.name: 1. Get Campaign by Event Tag
item.description: STEP 1 — Public. Client entry point. Returns active campaign details and active booth list. `campaignId` is auto-saved for subsequent requests.

response.name: 200 - Campaign + Booths
400 - Campaign Not Active
404 - Not Found

item.name: 2. Generate Raffle QR
item.description:
STEP 3 — Public. Client submits the booth codes scanned locally + optional participant info. Server validates codes, creates participant + scan logs + raffle QR atomically, and returns an encrypted QR.

`encryptedQr` is auto-saved for the next step.

request.description: All 5 active booths = 350pts ≥ threshold 300. `encryptedQr` is auto-saved.

response.name: 200 - QR Generated
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Raffle QR generated successfully",
  "data": {
    "raffleQrId": 1,
    "encryptedQr": "<url_safe_encrypted_string>",
    "participantId": 1,
    "participantCode": "CLIENT-DEVICE-ABC123",
    "totalPoints": 350,
    "thresholdPoints": 300,
    "boothCount": 5,
    "generatedAt": "2026-04-01T08:00:00.000Z"
  }
}

request.description: participantInfo is fully optional. A generated code (PART-N) is assigned.
2 booths = 125pts. Threshold is 300.

item.name: Generate Raffle QR — Invalid Booth Code ✗

request.description: Re-submit the same participantCode — should return 409.

item.name: 3. Validate Raffle
item.description:
STEP 5 — Public (raffle station). Decrypts and validates the QR, creates the raffle entry, marks QR as used. Returns `raffleEntryId` for the spin.

`raffleEntryId` and `participantId` are auto-saved.

request.description: Uses `encryptedQr` saved from Step 2. `raffleEntryId` and `participantId` are auto-saved.

response.name: 200 - Validated
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Raffle QR validated. Participant is eligible to spin.",
  "data": {
    "raffleEntryId": 1,
    "participantId": 1,
    "participantCode": "CLIENT-DEVICE-ABC123",
    "fullName": "Juan dela Cruz",
    "campaignId": 1,
    "totalPoints": 350,
    "thresholdPoints": 300,
    "status": "pending",
    "dateCreated": "2026-04-01T08:05:00.000Z"
  }
}

response.name: 409 - QR Already Used
400 - Tampered QR

request.description: Re-submit the same QR to confirm it cannot be used twice.

item.name: 4. Get Prizes (Wheel Catalog)
item.description: STEP 5 — Public. Load the prize list before rendering the wheel. Returns only active prizes ordered by sortOrder.

request.description: Public endpoint. Returns active prizes only. Use the returned `id` values as `prizeId` in spin-wheel.

response.name: 200 - Active Prizes

item.name: 5. Spin Wheel
item.description:
STEP 6 — Public (raffle station). The wheel spins client-side. Send the outcome here.

- **Win:** send `prizeId` (from the prizes catalog) + `wheelResult`
- **Lose:** omit `prizeId` or send `null`, set `wheelResult` to `"No Prize"`

Uses `raffleEntryId` auto-saved from Validate Raffle.

request.description: Send `prizeId` from the catalog. Server looks up the prize name, creates a claim record, and decrements quantity.

response.name: 200 - Win

request.description: Omit `prizeId` for a losing spin. No claim record is created. Entry marked as 'lost'.

response.name: 200 - No Prize

request.description: Re-spin the same entry after it has been processed.

item.name: 6. Validation Errors
item.description: Key Zod validation failure cases (no auth required — all public endpoints).

request.description: Hit an admin endpoint without a token — should return 401.

item.name: Get Campaign by Event Tag — Not Found ✗
F. Admin — Survey Management
item.description:
Full CRUD for surveys, questions, options, matrix rows, responses, and analytics.

All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{admin_token}}`.

Base path: `{{base_url}}/admin/campaigns/{{campaignId}}/surveys`

request.description: List all surveys for the campaign with active question count and total response count.
Returns the full survey with nested questions, options per question, and matrix rows.
Create a survey. `triggerEvent`: `booth_scan` | `raffle_entry` | `prize_claim`

body.raw:
{
  "surveyName": "Booth Visitor Feedback",
  "description": "Quick feedback after scanning the booth QR",
  "triggerEvent": "booth_scan",
  "isRequired": false,
  "isActive": true,
  "sortOrder": 0
}

request.description: Update any survey fields. All fields optional.
Delete survey. Blocked (409) if responses exist — deactivate with isActive=false instead.
List all questions for a survey.
Radio-button style question. Add selectable options via POST .../options after creating.
Checkbox style — participant can pick multiple options. Stored as answerJson array.
Star/numeric rating. validationRules.min and max define the allowed scale. Stored as answerNumeric.
Likert scale. Add options: Strongly Agree, Agree, Neutral, Disagree, Strongly Disagree via .../options.
Yes/No question. Add exactly 2 options (Yes, No) via .../options. Stored as answerOptionId.
Short free-text answer. Stored as answerText.
Paragraph/essay answer. Stored as answerText.
Numeric input. Stored as answerNumeric — supports AVG/SUM in analytics.
Date picker. Stored as answerDate (YYYY-MM-DD).
Drag-to-rank question. Add items via .../options. Stored as answerJson [{optionId, rank}].
Grid question. Add column headers via .../options and row sub-questions via .../matrix-rows. Stored as answerJson [{rowId, optionId}].
Update any question field. All fields optional.
Delete a question. Blocked (409) if answers exist — set isActive=false instead.
List all options for a question. Returns questionType so the client knows context.
Add a selectable option. Works for: single_choice, multiple_choice, dropdown, boolean, likert, ranking, and matrix (column headers).
Update an option. All fields optional.
Delete an option. Blocked (409) if answers reference it — set isActive=false instead.
List all row sub-questions for a matrix-type question. Returns 400 if question type is not matrix.
Add a row sub-question to a matrix question. Only valid for matrix-type questions.
Update a matrix row.
Delete a matrix row.
List all responses with participant info. Includes aggregate stats (total, completed, incomplete).

Per-question aggregated analytics.

- choice types → count + percentage per option
- rating/number → avg, min, max
- text/long_text → sample of last 20 answers
- complex JSON types → response count only

item.name: G. Admin — Analytics
item.description:
Campaign analytics dashboards. All requests require `Authorization: Bearer {{admin_token}}`.

Base path: `{{base_url}}/admin/analytics`

request.description: Counts all campaigns grouped by status (draft, active, paused, ended) plus grand total.
Participant breakdown by status, points distribution (avg/min/max), registration timeline by day.
Per-booth scan stats (total scans, unique participants, points distributed) + scan volume by day.
QR generation and usage stats: total generated, active, used, expired, conversion rate, avg seconds to use.
Spin wheel outcome breakdown (pending/won/lost/claimed), win rate, wheel result distribution, entries by day.
Prize inventory + claim breakdown per prize (won/claimed/forfeited), forfeiture rate, claims by day.
Full 7-step participant conversion funnel: Registered → Scanned → QR Generated → QR Validated → Wheel Spun → Won → Claimed. Includes drop-off % per step and overall conversion rate.

item.name: 7. Survey (Visitor)
item.description:
Visitor-facing survey endpoints. No authentication required.

**Flow after QR scan:**
1. `GET /campaigns/:campaignId/surveys/active?triggerEvent=booth_scan` → check for active survey
2. If `hasSurvey=true` → render questions in the UI
3. `GET .../response-status?participantId=X&scanLogId=Y` → skip if already answered
4. `POST .../submit` → submit answers atomically

request.description:
Fetch the active survey for a campaign by trigger event.

`hasSurvey=false` when no survey is configured — client should skip the survey step.

`triggerEvent` query param: `booth_scan` (default) | `raffle_entry` | `prize_claim`

Check if a participant has already submitted this survey for a given scan. Use to skip the survey UI without re-asking.

Query params: `participantId` (required), `scanLogId` (optional)

Submit survey answers atomically. All required questions must be answered.

**Answer column by questionType:**

| Type | Column | Example |
|---|---|---|
| single_choice, dropdown, boolean, likert | `answerOptionId` | `3` |
| rating, number | `answerNumeric` | `4.5` |
| text, long_text | `answerText` | `"Great event"` |
| date | `answerDate` | `"2026-04-08"` |
| multiple_choice | `answerJson` | `[{optionId:5},{optionId:8}]` |
| ranking | `answerJson` | `[{optionId:3,rank:1},{optionId:7,rank:2}]` |
| matrix | `answerJson` | `[{rowId:1,optionId:4},{rowId:2,optionId:5}]` |

**Idempotent** — re-submitting same `participantId+scanLogId` returns the already-completed response.
Example for a `multiple_choice` question — `answerJson` is an array of selected optionIds.
Example for a `ranking` question — `answerJson` is an array of `{optionId, rank}` ordered by rank ascending.
Expect 400 — answers array must not be empty.
Re-submit the same participantId + scanLogId. Returns 200 with alreadySubmitted=true instead of creating a duplicate.
